summary: Review API Management services for gateway, backend, hostname, subscription, and named-value posture that can mask the true public-to-backend path.

targets.masking_reason: public gateway, backend indirection, and API/consumer contract posture are visible; current identity has visible APIM route or backend policy control

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Clients see the APIM hostname instead of the backend service path.
capability_steps.boundary: Gateway posture does not prove traffic volume.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Shows where the published API surface can forward requests behind the gateway.
capability_steps.boundary: Backend hostname posture does not prove ownership or reachability.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Can rewrite paths, switch backends, normalize auth, or keep the true route opaque to callers.
capability_steps.boundary: Policy XML bodies are not collected by default.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Existing products and subscriptions can keep caller behavior normal while the backend path stays abstracted.
capability_steps.boundary: Consumer use and request contents require runtime logs.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Normal cover stories include API versioning, throttling, partner exposure, backend migration, and failover.
capability_steps.boundary: Cover story is not an intent claim.
